commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh A slothful man hides his hand in his dish, and will not so much as bring it to his mouth again. Or Hebrew tsallachath, "in the pot "out of which he eats; which shows his negligence. Mouth, to eat. This hyperbole indicates that he is too lazy to take the necessary sustenance, chap. (Calmet) Septuagint, "in his breast unjustly. Neither will he bring them to his mouth. "(Haydock) He will bestow nothing, and shall reap no benefit from his avarice. Bernard) He will not practise what he teaches. Gregory, Mor.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
